description: '''Odumodu''' is a traditional [[folk music]] genre of the [[Igbo people]] of southeastern [[Nigeria]], originating among the [[Ohuhu]] people of [[Umuahia]] in [[Abia State]]. It is predominantly performed by men, featuring a lead vocalist who drives call-and-response exchanges with a chorus of male backing singers who often double as instrumentalists. The genre draws on traditional Igbo percussion instruments and is characterized by its layered rhythmic textures, harmonized chants, wise sayings, and storytelling content. Odumodu is performed at a wide range of community ceremonies and festivals, serving social, celebratory, and commemorative functions.
